job: Close every finding and remaining action in PORTABILITY-AUDIT.md.
contract_assumptions: The audit document is the source of truth for scope.
The user authorized local evidence vendoring and the first repository commit by asking to fix everything in the audit.
The OpenAI release artifact is a claim-focused evidence extract, not a byte-for-byte upstream snapshot, because direct archival retrieval returned HTTP 403.
inspected: repository source, tests, generated bindings, evidence catalogs, and validation controls
the seven local source artifacts named by the audit
the three primary web references named by catalog 1.1.0

evidence.preexisting_hashes: negative-parallelism report and all four Solene artifacts matched their recorded sha256 values before promotion
evidence.new_baselines: Clarvoy source and ingested Markdown first hashed 2026-07-17; web snapshots first hashed 2026-07-17
evidence.frozen_provenance: catalog 1.0.0 remains byte-identical to the hash in the cycle-0010 build receipt

tests.result: pass: skills 6/6; surface sync; 10/10 evidence artifacts offline; portability 15/15; verifier 12/12; e2e 14/14; pytest 150 passed with 6 subtests

ai_output_review.adjudication: Both uncertain 'rather than' matches are legitimate technical contrasts; no source rewrite was accepted.

source_preservation: verified by the analyzer receipt input hash and by the frozen catalog regression test

external_effects.network_note: One primary-source browsing batch, seven explicit curl retrieval attempts, and one uv dependency synchronization. Some OpenAI curl attempts returned HTTP 403.

limits: The OpenAI evidence extract preserves only the catalog claims and retrieval provenance; it is not a full upstream page snapshot.
Engineering verification does not establish empirical model efficacy, product demand, or production readiness.
